title: Remove Consecutive Duplicates from a String with Edge Cases (Case Sensitive)

statement.markdown: Given a string, remove consecutive duplicates from it. For example, for input `abcdabcdefgef`, the output should be `abcdefg`. Also consider edge cases like empty string, string with only one character, string with only duplicates etc. The function should return the modified string.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the modified string
statement.notes: The function should be able to handle strings with length up to 10000

examples.explanation: Explanation: Consecutive duplicates removed from the input string

hints.text: Use a two pointers approach to remove consecutive duplicates

editorial.title: Editorial: Remove Consecutive Duplicates from a String
editorial.intuition: The idea is to use a two pointers approach where we keep a pointer to the last non-duplicate character
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would involve creating a new string with no duplicates
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution involves using a two pointers approach to remove consecutive duplicates in linear time
commonMistakes: Not counting the edge cases correctly
